Fixed the queue loss bug in [src/async_queue.py](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py). The key change is that [`AsyncBoundedQueue.put()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py#L47-L59) now awaits `self._queue.put(item)` instead of using `put_nowait()` and returning `False` when full, so producers properly backpressure instead of dropping messages. I also moved dequeue accounting into [`get()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py#L61-L76) and removed the helper consumer’s direct mutation so counts stay consistent.

I also tightened [`drain()`](file:///c:/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_011_amp-deep3_tukg0nkz/src/async_queue.py#L82-L99) so it waits for the queue to stay empty across an event-loop turn, which lets it flush items released by blocked putters during bulk operations like `put_many()`.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` → `7 passed in 0.51s`.

Fixed the stale memoization issue by changing cache key generation in [src/memoize.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_b1g_yckx/src/memoize.py#L8-L53) from object-identity-based keys (`id(...)`) to a recursive frozen representation of argument content. Lists, tuples, dict-like mappings, sets, and objects with `__dict__` are now converted into stable hashable values before caching, so equal mutable inputs share a cache entry and later mutations produce a different key instead of returning stale results.

I also added a focused regression test in [tests/test_memoize.py](file:///c%3A/Users/danie/AppData/Local/Temp/bench_task_012_amp-deep3_b1g_yckx/tests/test_memoize.py#L65-L76) to cover nested mutable keyword data, alongside the existing list and dict cases.
